title: Rich Whole30 Ethiopian Lamb Kitfo
description: A rich and complex lamb kitfo dish using hojicha for added depth of flavor. Perfect for a quick and easy dinner or meal prep.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned.
Transfer the lamb to a plate and set aside to rest.
In the same skillet, add the hojicha matcha powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cilantro and parsley to the skillet and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Add the black pepper and sea salt to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the lamb back to the skillet and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
Sprinkle the unflavored gelatin over the top of the lamb and continue to cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let cool slightly.
Transfer the lamb to a cutting board and slice into thin strips.
Serve the kitfo on a tagliere, garnished with additional cilantro and parsley if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze the kitfo for later use.
To serve, let the kitfo come to room temperature and slice thinly.
Serve the kitfo with your choice of sides, such as sautéed vegetables or a side salad.
Note: Kitfo is typically served with a raw egg yolk on top, but since this recipe is whole30 compliant, we will be omitting the egg yolk.
Instead, we will be serving the kitfo with a sprinkle of fresh cilantro and parsley.
This will add a bright and refreshing flavor to the dish without compromising the whole30 diet.
